campaign: A4 — Cost/concurrency budget + revert path + reclaim (trust-first guardrails)

phase_outcomes.P2: Fast revert path. A one-command revert of a landed_sha rides the SAME verify-gated merge train (main never breaks reverting — verify runs against the rebased tree before FF). Server has NO git + the responder tail is escalation-welded, so adjudicated: INTEGRATOR-produces. REST POST /projects/{id}/merge-requests/revert {landedSha,resource?,reason?,escalationId?} (any-authed, mirror 7.4 break-glass) → submitRevert inserts a branchless queued MR {taskId:null, branch:null, revertOf:landedSha, escalationId} (copies an existing landed MR's verifyCmd for the same sha, else project default). The integrator materializes the branch at the EXISTING null-branch-safe pickup seam (loop.ts: after baseSha, the `ref=branch??commitSha; if(!ref)reject` point; batch.ts: after reset, before computeSpeculativeBase, reset-back-to-clean-main) PRESENCE-GUARDED on revertOf!=null&&branch==null: gitOps.revert(sha) (git revert --no-edit, conflict→--abort→reject; +createBranch helper) → push pm/revert-<sha> → set branch → everything downstream byte-identical. DETERMINISTIC (no LLM → no injection surface). revertOf column (migration 0033_bumpy_apocalypse, PLAIN nullable text NOT an FK — a landed sha possibly landed out-of-band) threaded through row/view/list-filter + @pm/shared zod-3 + route zod-4 mirror + openapi/web regen; migration-journal.test LAST_REVERT/LAST_COLUMN_CHECK bumped. Queryable audit chain (?revertOf=sha) + timeline renders it; reuses the A2 escalation post-back (land→resolve, reject→needs_human). Single-sha v1 (arc-level=N calls, parked). server 1721 (+9), integrator 329 (+5), shared 514 (+1). build/typecheck/lint + openapi/web regen green. ef52b48.
phase_outcomes.P1: Spend/concurrency budget — two NEW server-derived caps extending the existing spawn budget (canSpawn/recordSpawn/slot pool reused unchanged). (1) max concurrent ARCS: an admission gate in the systemic branch BEFORE runDriveSession (no epic created on hold) — escalateToHuman when inFlightArcs>=cap; arc count = a FRESH per-tick probe over the FULL listAcknowledgedByHolder list (pendingDrive||pendingArc && !arcComplete), GATED on autoImplementEnabled so answer-mode does zero new fetches (byte-identical). (2) max arc DURATION: a cap in advanceArc AFTER the arc_complete check (so an all-landed-this-cycle arc completes, not caps) — arcStartedAtMs reads the durable P3 pendingDrive intent-marker createdAt (fail-safe null⇒never caps); on cap → the existing arc_partial escalate (landed phases preserved on main, NO rollback — revert is P2). Both server-derived ⇒ restart-resilient. Config: autoImplement.budget{maxConcurrentArcs=100, maxArcDurationSec=604800} + 2 env vars (positiveInt); generous defaults ⇒ A1-A3 byte-identical. Noted honestly: a fixed tick-start snapshot can over-admit at maxConcurrent>1 (default 1 avoids it; cost governor not safety gate — train verify is the floor). 178 responder tests (+8). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false. 8b8ac5e.

notes: A4 start. Depends on A2 (escalation-linked train land) + A3 (autonomous multi-phase drive — DONE @ 8460ca0). Trust-first guardrails: governance + insurance, NOT human-approval gates. P1 = spend/concurrency budget (max concurrent arcs + max arc duration) extending the existing spawn budget; exhaustion → pause+escalate. P2 = revert path (undo a landed_sha through the verify-gated train). P3 = reclaim stranded drives + submitted-but-unlanded MRs. P4 = metrics data for A5 + seal/docs/close. Ships enabled=false.
